text.title: Feedback Examples, App Store Strategy, and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Monthly review example, app store response strategy, critical antipatterns, and human-in-loop checkpoints for feedback pipelines.

example.note: Monthly review with actionable output for each major category
example.code:
Total feedback this month: 150 items Top category: Integrations (40%) Top request: Slack integration (25 mentions across 18 unique users) Emerging pattern: Mobile app requests up 3x vs last month
Actions: - Slack integration: added to Q2 roadmap; emailed 25 requesters - Mobile: insufficient data for roadmap; scheduled 6 user interviews - Billing complaints: 3 bugs fixed in current sprint
Close-loop sent: 25 emails for Slack roadmap addition Feedback→roadmap conversion rate this month: 8% (target >5%)

list.item: 1-2 star reviews: respond within 24h, apologize, offer direct support channel, log in feedback system
3-4 star reviews: thank and ask what would make it 5 stars, log the gap
5 star reviews: thank and ask for referrals or testimonial

rule.statement: Run a quarterly drift audit: sample 50 LLM-categorized items, have a human re-tag, measure agreement rate. If below 80%, retrain or update the prompt.
rule.rationale: LLM categorization drifts as product evolves and language patterns change. Without periodic audit, the taxonomy gradually loses meaning.

rule.statement: Use embedding similarity (cosine >= 0.85) for deduplication, not full-text match. Paraphrases of the same issue are the same issue.
rule.rationale: Naive full-text dedup misses paraphrases. Counting "search is slow" and "search takes forever" as two separate requests undercounts the true signal by half.

antipattern.description: Sending raw feedback verbatim with emails or account IDs to LLM provider.
antipattern.reason: PII in LLM calls may violate GDPR DPA. Strip emails, phone numbers, account IDs with a regex pre-pass or dedicated scrubber agent before any LLM call.

antipattern.description: Using raw vote counts from Canny or Productboard as prioritization input.
antipattern.reason: Vote count correlates poorly with retention impact. One churned enterprise customer who never voted matters more than 50 free users who all voted.

antipattern.description: Promoting a feedback cluster to roadmap item without human review of the decision.
antipattern.reason: Feedback volume may reflect vocal minority, not majority pain. Human checkpoint before roadmap promotion prevents shipping for the wrong users.

section.title: Human-in-Loop Checkpoints

rule.statement: Three mandatory human checkpoints: (1) before any outbound message, (2) before promoting feedback cluster to roadmap item, (3) before changing taxonomy.
rule.rationale: Each of these has irreversible effects: sent email cannot be unsent, roadmap items create expectations, taxonomy changes invalidate historical analysis.

section.reference: Three response templates: feature shipped, not planned, more info needed.
